title: Bike Touring Vlog - Germany - Up Rhine From Mainz To End of Rhine. 4-2010

transcript.text: So here I am in Worms, Germany, and the Rhine is still there and still has a bike path.
The Church and Stair is the bike path.
The bike path, kilometers north of Giermanstein.
Look they've got one wall. It's pretty cool.
Check out my campsite. I forgot to do the filming of my last night's campsite, which was kind of silly.
But I'm in the woods. That hill right there on the other side of it.
This is where the bike path is.
I'm totally excluded from anything. Secluded.
In the middle of the woods.
I just saw a couple of deer chasing each other and one of them going, Arrrr!
That's kind of strange.
Hey, happy 420.
I think I'll do a little update video.
Let's see here.
I rode from Cologne and then ever since the Cologne I've been riding up the Rhine.
And there have been bike paths up the whole Rhine pretty much and that's been cool.
You hit towns, you know, quite often and then I've been getting lost at the town.
But I've been getting better at finding the bike path.
You just have to keep a really close eye out for all the signs and for the bike paths and just kind of read the mind of how the people who put the signs there on the roads and everything.
Right now I'm on a really cool section.
It's a really nice bike path pretty much along the Rhine but you're going through like a forest.
So I just, all I had to do is walk off the bike path for a little bit and now I'm all by myself in some cool forest.
So that's really cool.
I'm just south of a town called, I can't even remember, a few miles south of like Wyrm.
And so I rode up the Rhine and happened to actually stay at the house of my friend, my YouTube friend, David.
And that was cool.
Stayed one night.
Should've stayed too though.
Cause it was fun.
We were just hanging out in the, in his basement watching, showing his, you know, his cool YouTube videos.
He's got like his own little website he made collecting all the different YouTube videos and stuff.
And, um, so now we're going to exchange like MP3s and stuff.
I give him all my MP3s.
Um, and then, my road down south.
Oh man, I guess talking makes your throat rot.
Man, I wasn't coughing before.
Okay.
So, um, yeah, my legs are kind of sore though.
My legs have been sore for a few days now.
Um, so I think tomorrow, um, I'm going to, I'm going to experiment with riding all day.
But, but not riding hard.
Like recovery riding and see if day after tomorrow I can be totally fresh.
Because I'd like to have at least one totally fresh day a week.
You know, I don't want to be sore constantly, um, every single day.
That's kind of one thing I learned from weight lifting last year is you definitely want to recover.
Because if not, you never do and then you just don't get stronger.
So, um, plus I want to see what it's like, you know, active recovery.
See if I can actually recover by riding my bike all day.
Hmm.
Because, you know, it kind of gets hard to just ride easy.
You always want to ride hard.
So, I mean, just like naturally.
So, uh, we'll see how that goes.
Plus, it's, it's a, it's a great terrain to ride easy because you've got the bike pads, you know, and all the different turns and stuff.
And then after this, it's Switzerland.
You know, I don't know if there's bike pads there, but there's hills and stuff.
So, um, it'll be easier to burn myself out.
I'll burn myself out there.
Um, I took my bike into the bike shop today because my rear wheel was really wobbly, like loose.
The guy at Cologne didn't fix it right and I told him, I was like, look, it's still loose.
He's like, oh, it's, it's like naturally like that.
But the gears weren't changing, right?
So, I took it in the bike shop today and it was still wobbly when he took it out, but he said he replaced the ball bearings.
And I, I checked the, the change, the gear shifting and it's perfect now.
So, even though it's wobbly, the gear shifting is perfect.
So, I'm happy.
I bought a new pump because I left my pump in the bathroom the other day when I was fixing a flat.
And I got more tubes because I don't like having tubes of flats because I got a flat the other day.
Um, while I was riding, I was, luckily I was only going like 10 miles an hour.
10 or 15 miles an hour.
And it happened because it was one of those flats where all of a sudden you're flat.
And I almost crashed even only going 10 miles an hour because, you know, I have that big front bag on.
So, I mean, if I'm going, so I will go down if I like bust a sudden flat.
And I think I got that flat because it was from a, it was because I had an old tube.
Because, uh, my, uh, one of my patches on the tube like got folded or something.
And so, that's why I want to make sure my tubes in there are really, next time I'm going really fast like down a hillside.
Make sure I have fresh tubes in there so that they don't just pop on me and make me go down.
Yeah, so I had three beers at the thing, at the, at the restaurant today and they had internet.
That was cool.
But, um, I said the bill and she brought me a beer.
So, bill rhymes with beer.
So, um, I think I'll have to not say, they said the other word but I can't remember.
So, I'll just hold my money up and say pay so they don't bring me a beer.
Because beers are expensive.
Well, they're not expensive in Germany comparatively but they're expensive, you know.
They might get some calories, it's not that good.
These Germans looked at me today and they go,
The more beer you drink, the harder it would be to ride your bike.
I'm like, I know.
It was an accident to get the third one.
But, um, anyways, um, I'll be on the Rhine for the next couple days.
I'm looking forward to that.
And tomorrow I'll give a tour of all the stuff that I have.
Okay, ciao, ciao.
